questions.question: The types of problems that can be solved based on evolutionary computation are: 1. Optimization problems; 2. Search problems in the solution space; 3. Autonomous vehicle navigation

answers.b: 1,4,6 (See course I: Introduction: - Slide 9 and 10)

questions.question: In an evolutionary algorithm, the quality function: 1. Evaluates the quality of the algorithm; 2. Evaluates the quality of each candidate; 3. Evaluates the speed of finding the solution relative to resource consumption; 4. Must be maximized; 5. Determines if a descendant is acceptable; 6. Selects the individuals that will reproduce; 7. Selects the individuals that pass to the next generation; 8. Contains a random factor; 9. Evaluates the quality of the current population; 10. Evaluates the quality of the current population compared to the previous generation;

questions.question: The components of evolutionary algorithms are: 1. Representation; 2. Mutation probability; 3. Evaluation function; 4. Crossover probability; 5. Population; 6. Random number generation; 7. Parent selection mechanism; 8. Permutation generation; 9. Graphical representation of quality evolution; 10. Variation operators; 11. Establishing genetic diversity of the population; 12. Mechanism for replacing the current population; 13. Hillclimbing; 14. Population initialization; 15. Termination condition;
Evolutionary computation is inspired by

answers.b: The knowledge society
answers.e: Biological natural evolution (See course I: Introduction - Slide 6)

questions.question: The Hillclimbing algorithm: 1. Applies to a single point in the search space; 2. Application can be repeated for multiple points to increase performance; 3. Is inspired by mountaineering techniques; 4. Always finds the optimal solution; 5. Sometimes finds the optimal solution; 6. Calculations end when the system temperature becomes 0; 7. Usually finds a local optimum point; 8. Is used only for representation with real number strings;

The characteristics of a classic (canonical) genetic algorithm are: 1. Population representation is performed via binary strings; 2. Population representation is performed via natural number strings; 3. The probability of selecting an individual into the parent multiset is proportional to its evaluation function value; 4. The probability of selecting an individual into the parent multiset is given by the individual's rank position in the population, determined based on the evaluation function; 5. The probability of performing a mutation is small; 6. The probability of performing a mutation is high; 7.The probability of performing recombination is low; 8. The probability of performing recombination is high; 9. The replacement of the current population is based on age.

Edge Recombination Crossover; 25. Cyclic Recombination

answers.b: Is performed with a low probability
answers.g: Selects, on average, half of the individuals for mutation
answers.h: Is performed only once during an algorithm
answers.j: Is performed immediately upon availability of a child population

answers.e: Is performed immediately before every mutation procedure
answers.f: Is performed immediately after every parent selection stage
answers.g: Is used with a high probability
answers.i: Is used only in unconstrained problems
